summary.intent: Implement task #292: build the DateTimePicker agnostic core state machine for a Rust UI component library, fusing segmented date input, segmented time input, and a calendar popover into one machine producing a CalendarDateTime value.
summary.outcome: Fully implemented DateTimePicker (mod.rs + 120 tests + 34 snapshots), promoted hour_cycle helpers to a shared module, introduced a canonical CLDR-backed date_field_separator function replacing three divergent heuristic duplicates, synced heavily drifted spec, added mandatory conformance and proptest entries, passed all xci-fast gates at 90.1% branch coverage, and committed/pushed the PR.

repo: Spec files for date-picker and time-field had been migrated to typed Effect enum + PendingEffect::named() convention, but date-time-picker.md had not — spec drift must be caught and fixed in the same PR.
KeyboardKey has no digit variants; digit input uses KeyboardKey::Unidentified with the character carried separately.
CalendarDate does not derive Ord, so '<'/'>' comparison won't compile — must use CalendarDate::compare.

Date separator heuristic was duplicated across DatePicker (default_format_for_locale), DateField (literal_for_locale), and any new consumer, with a ko-KR divergence (DatePicker used '.' while DateField used '. ').
date_order uses a canonical 3-tier resolution (icu4x → web-intl → heuristic fallback); date_field_separator now mirrors this pattern exactly.
SegmentPatternPart::Literal was &'static str and Copy; accepting CLDR-backed owned separators required converting it to String and dropping Copy.
MessageFn equality is pointer-based (Arc closure), so two independently-built Messages instances are never equal — equality tests must clone one context rather than build two.

ars-components has a 90% line / 90% branch coverage gate (xtask/src/coverage.rs); manual PartialEq &&-chains across 25+ fields each contribute ~2 untestable branch arms and can silently erode branch coverage.
Tuples only impl PartialEq up to 12 elements; splitting a large &&-chain into tuple comparisons requires keeping each tuple ≤12 fields.
Spec conformance tests and proptest state machine entries are mandatory for every new agnostic component — siblings (DateField, DatePicker, TimeField) all have both.
Adapter specs (Dioxus, Leptos) propagated the nonexistent DateTime type instead of CalendarDateTime — adapter spec drift must be corrected in the implementing PR.
aria-describedby belongs on Control (the focusable trigger), not on HiddenInput (type=hidden, non-focusable to AT).
workspace-wide 'cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject' is authoritative; per-package '-p' runs produce false positives because feature-gated tests don't run and their snapshots appear unreferenced.

code.finding: New date_field_separator() function mirrors date_order's 3-tier resolution: icu4x probe → web-intl → fallback heuristic. Shared separator_from_formatted() parser reuses the same digit-normalization as the field-order probe.
literal_for_locale() was the DateField-specific separator heuristic (now deleted). SegmentPatternPart::Literal changed from &'static str to String; Copy was dropped from the derive.
default_format_for_locale() was the DatePicker-specific separator heuristic (now replaced by first char of date_field_separator()).
ComponentIds::part() produces '{base}-{part}' with a hyphen; ComponentIds::id() returns the base id.
MessageFn wraps Arc closures; equality is pointer-based, so two fresh Services built from the same Messages constructor are never equal. Clone the Context to test equality.
Manual PartialEq &&-chain across 25 Context fields contributed ~48 branch misses. Replaced with grouped tuple comparisons (≤12 fields each) to move branch arms into core and recover coverage.

workflow: Run 'cargo insta test --workspace --unreferenced=reject' (not '-p <crate>') to replicate the CI snapshot gate; per-package runs give false positives for feature-gated tests.
cargo xci-fast is the pre-push gate (~10-15 min); run it before committing to catch coverage and clippy regressions.
Branch coverage is harder to move than line coverage — plan for multiple iterations when a module has many boolean guards or large match arms.
Post-implementation audit phases: (1) spec/impl drift, (2) iterative breadth sweep (conformance, proptest, adapter specs, prelude, CI matrix) until two consecutive clean rounds, (3) coverage gate.
Background bash tasks for long-running gates plus a monitor/wait loop avoids blocking the main context on multi-minute builds.
When a coverage gate fails, get the raw lcov per-file branch miss report ('cargo llvm-cov --lcov') and map line numbers to source to target only reachable branches.

friction: The spec (date-time-picker.md §1.3/§1.7/§1.8) was heavily drifted from the real API: nonexistent DateTime type, non-Ord date comparison with '<'/'>', wrong PendingEffect API, wrong NonZero month arity, wrong aria_label/Time::new arities — required a near-complete spec rewrite.
'cargo insta accept' does not take a '-p' flag, causing initial confusion about orphaned snapshots; investigation revealed they were feature-gated tests not run in the default invocation.
Branch coverage required five separate iterations (89.8% → 89.9% → 90.0%[rounded, still failing] → 90.0%[still failing] → 90.1%) before passing the strict gate.
The 25-field manual PartialEq &&-chain was the primary branch coverage blocker; diagnosing it required lcov analysis and a refactor to tuple comparisons.
Markdown table alignment broke when adding a new row because '→' is multi-byte, confusing column width calculations; required ASCII substitution and manual padding.
SegmentPatternPart::Literal was Copy + &'static str, requiring a non-trivial type change (to String, dropping Copy) across DateField's pattern machinery to support owned CLDR separators.
MessageFn Arc equality caused an equality test to fail silently (both contexts compared unequal despite identical props); fix required cloning one context rather than building two.
open_items: DateField spec's LocaleSeparators per-position abstraction is a pre-existing design (separate separator for each position in the date pattern) that goes beyond what date_field_separator() currently provides — full realization deferred.
Pre-existing cargo doc intra-doc-link warnings in the unrelated table component; not touched and still present on main.
Branch coverage for dtp mod.rs cleared the gate at 90.1% — a very thin margin; future changes to the machine's guard logic could regress it without additional test investment.
The 3-tier CLDR separator resolution (icu4x → web-intl → fallback) is fully wired but only exercised under the 'i18n' feature in CI; the web-intl (WASM) path has no dedicated integration test.
